description: You are an IT-support technician who has just received ticket “INC10234”. The customer needs TCP port 9000 opened on the company’s application servers. All firewall rules are kept in a plain-text list that another automation service later converts into real iptables entries, so **no root privileges are required**; you only have to edit the rule file that lives inside the shared support directory. 1. File that must be modified • Path: /home/user/it_support/firewall/allow_rules.list • Current content (exactly): ``` # Firewall Allow Rules ALLOW_TCP 22 ALLOW_TCP 443 ``` • Desired final content (exactly – including the two pre-existing lines and one new line): ``` # Firewall Allow Rules ALLOW_TCP 22 ALLOW_TCP 443 ALLOW_TCP 9000 # Ticket INC10234 ``` Notes: a) The new line must be appended **as shown**, with one space after “ALLOW_TCP”, then the port number, one single space, “#”, a single space, and the ticket number. b) Do **not** alter the order or spacing of the first two lines. 2. Log file that must be created • Path: /home/user/it_support/logs/INC10234_action.log • It must contain exactly these four lines (including the final newline): ``` Timestamp: 2023-10-01 09:30:00 Action: Added firewall rule Rule: ALLOW_TCP 9000 Status: Success ``` Do **not** add extra spaces or blank lines. Verification is automated. The tests will confirm that: • /home/user/it_support/firewall/allow_rules.list matches the three-line content shown above **verbatim**. • /home/user/it_support/logs/INC10234_action.log exists and matches the four-line content shown above **verbatim**. Once these two conditions are satisfied the ticket is considered resolved.
truth:
Initial state provided to the agent:

Directory structure:
  /home/user/it_support/
      firewall/
          allow_rules.list   (contains exactly the 3 lines shown in the task’s “Current content” block)
      logs/                 (directory exists and is writable)

Files & expected final content for grading:

1. /home/user/it_support/firewall/allow_rules.list
   Expected final content:
   "# Firewall Allow Rules\nALLOW_TCP 22\nALLOW_TCP 443\nALLOW_TCP 9000 # Ticket INC10234\n"

2. /home/user/it_support/logs/INC10234_action.log
   Expected final content:
   "Timestamp: 2023-10-01 09:30:00\nAction: Added firewall rule\nRule: ALLOW_TCP 9000\nStatus: Success\n"
